pipeorgans.ponder.pipe_adjusting.text_1: Pipes can be placed on Windchests and Fluid Tanks
pipeorgans.ponder.pipe_adjusting.text_2: If the Windchest is configured correctly...
pipeorgans.ponder.pipe_adjusting.text_3: ...or, alternatively, if the tank receives sufficient heat...
pipeorgans.ponder.pipe_adjusting.text_4: ...the Pipe will play a note when activated
pipeorgans.ponder.pipe_adjusting.text_5: Use the corresponding Pipe item on the block to lower its pitch
pipeorgans.ponder.pipe_adjusting.text_6: Cycle between five different octaves using a Wrench
pipeorgans.ponder.pipe_adjusting.text_7: Engineer's Goggles can help to find out the current pitch and octave of a Pipe
pipeorgans.ponder.pipe_swapping.text_1: You can instantly change the type of a Pipe by using another Pipe's item on it
pipeorgans.ponder.pipe_swapping.text_2: However, if the new pipe is too long, blocks can get in the way
pipeorgans.ponder.pipe_swapping.text_3: To fix this, simply remove any blocks that are in the way.
pipeorgans.ponder.pipe_swapping.text_4: The same goes for horizontal pipes
pipeorgans.ponder.tracker_bar.text_1: The tracker bar is used to play MIDI files through music rolls, which you can create with the Roll Authoring Table
pipeorgans.ponder.tracker_bar.text_2: When the tracker bar plays a music roll, it sends out a redstone signal to the associated first frequency on a Redstone link
pipeorgans.ponder.tracker_bar.text_3: The item associated with a frequency corresponds to an instrument used by the MIDI file
pipeorgans.ponder.tracker_bar.text_4: Then it sends out the note to play on the second frequency, which you can find a chart for online
pipeorgans.ponder.windchests.text_1: Windchests allow you to toggle several pipes at once
pipeorgans.ponder.windchests.text_2: The Windchests must be placed with the red arrow pointing toward the Windchest Controller
pipeorgans.ponder.windchests.text_3: Then place a fan blowing air into the Windchest Controller
pipeorgans.ponder.windchests.text_4: When the Controller is powered by redstone...
pipeorgans.ponder.windchests.text_6: Now, any pipes attached to the Windchests will sound when powered by redstone
pipeorgans.ponder.windchests.text_7: ... as long as the Windchest Controller has power
